scenario: BPMN XML has containing , , etc. Team wonders the role of extension elements.
question: BPMN extension elements concept?

options.text: Extension elements = vendor-specific additions to standard BPMN: the <bpmn:extensionElements> container allows engine vendors to attach custom configuration to any BPMN element without violating the standard schema. Camunda 8 uses the zeebe: namespace for elements such as zeebe:taskDefinition, zeebe:ioMapping, zeebe:assignmentDefinition, and zeebe:formDefinition. Other engines use their own namespaces. This mechanism enables engine-specific behavior while keeping the BPMN process portable at the logical model level, though portability is limited when extensions contain engine-specific details.

Extension elements in BPMN are ignored by the engine at runtime and serve only as documentation -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. Camunda 8 Zeebe actively reads and processes extension elements to configure execution behavior. Without zeebe:taskDefinition, a Service Task lacks a job type and cannot be activated by workers. Without zeebe:ioMapping, input and output variable mappings do not occur. Extension elements are essential configuration that directly governs how the engine executes each BPMN element.

Extension elements are specific to Camunda and are not part of the BPMN standard -- this partially misses the point. 0 as a standard extension mechanism allowing any vendor to attach custom configuration. Camunda 7 used the camunda: namespace, while Camunda 8 uses zeebe:. Any BPMN engine can define its own extension namespace. The mechanism is standardized; the namespace content is vendor-specific.

a.text: Incorrect. Extension elements: BPMN spec design: Standard elements: tasks, gateways, events, etc. Standard attributes: id, name, etc. Vendor extensions: via container. Why extensions exist: BPMN is executable spec (level 3) + notation. Engine implementations differ: need engine-specific config. Common pattern: vendor namespace. Camunda 8 extensions (zeebe namespace): zeebe:taskDefinition (Service Task): xml zeebe:ioMapping (Input/Output): xml zeebe:assignmentDefinition (User Task): xml zeebe:formDefinition (User Task form): xml zeebe:calledElement (Call Activity): xml zeebe:taskHeaders (Task Headers): xml zeebe:loopCharacteristics (Multi-Instance): xml And many more. Vendor namespaces: zeebe:: Camunda 8 (Zeebe engine). camunda:: Camunda 7 (legacy). Other engines: own namespaces. Portability: With extensions: BPMN file engine-specific. Pure BPMN (no extensions): portable but less expressive. For migration between engines: Camunda 7 → 8: change namespaces + adjust attributes. Camunda 8 → other engine: significant translation needed. For tooling: Modelers: Camunda Web Modeler: native zeebe extensions. bpmn.io editor: supports extensions via plugins. Validators: Engine validators: ensure extensions correct. Linters: bpmnlint: extension-aware rules (per Set 14 Q59). For Element Templates (per Set 13 Q51): Generate extensions: from modeler UI. Encapsulate complexity: modeler doesn't write XML. For documentation generators: Read extensions: extract config. Generate docs: per element. For diff / merge (per Set 16 Q59): Extensions diff: meaningful changes. Some changes: visual (DI); some logical (zeebe). For engine version compatibility: Older Zeebe versions: may not support newer extensions. BPMN deployment: validate against engine version. For BPMN engine of choice: Each engine: own extension namespace. Same logical process: different XML. Standard BPMN-only modeling: Pros: Portable across engines. Cons: Engine config lacking. Need to add at deployment. Hybrid: Logical BPMN: standard. Engine config: external (deployment descriptor). For Camunda 8 specifically: Zeebe extensions necessary: for execution. Lock-in: deliberately. For learning: Read BPMN XML: see zeebe extensions. Understand structure: useful for debugging. For tools generating BPMN: Templating: include extensions. Validation: post-generation. For the scenario (zeebe extensions in BPMN): Standard BPMN extension mechanism. Vendor namespace for Camunda 8. Configures engine behavior for elements. Without extensions: Pure BPMN: doesn't execute on Camunda 8. Need taskDefinition etc.: for Service Tasks.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
